sectiondef.header: Interrupt Status and Enable Register bitmaps and masks

description.para: Bit definitions for the interrupt status register and interrupt enable registers.

sectiondef.header: Global Interrupt Enable Register bitmaps and masks

description.para: Bit definitions for the Global Interrupt Enable register

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each GPIO device in the system.
This table contains configuration information for each GPIO device in the system.
Initializes the XGpio instance provided by the caller based on the given configuration data.

detaileddescription.para: Nothing is done except to initialize the InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an XGpio instance. The memory the pointer references must be pre-allocated by the caller. Further calls to manipulate the driver through the XGpio API must be made with this pointer.

Reference to a structure containing information about a specific GPIO device. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config. This function can initialize multiple instance objects with the use of multiple calls giving different Config information on each call.

Device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, use Config->BaseAddress for this parameters, passing the physical address instead.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the initialization is successful.

briefdescription.para: Sets the input/output direction of all discrete signals for the specified GPIO channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an XGpio instance to be worked on.
Contains the channel of the GPIO (1 or 2) to operate on.
Bitmask specifying which discretes are input and which are output. Bits set to 0 are output and bits set to 1 are input.

simplesect.para: The hardware must be built for dual channels if this function is used with any channel other than 1. If it is not, this function will assert.

briefdescription.para: Gets the input/output direction of all discrete signals for the specified GPIO channel.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to an XGpio instance to be worked on.
Channel
Contains the channel of the GPIO (1 or 2) to operate on.
Bitmask specifying which discretes are input and which are output. Bits set to 0 are output and bits set to 1 are input.
The hardware must be built for dual channels if this function is used with any channel other than 1. If it is not, this function will assert.

briefdescription.para: Reads state of discretes for the specified GPIO channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an XGpio instance to be worked on.
Contains the channel of the GPIO (1 or 2) to operate on.

simplesect.para: Current copy of the discretes register.
The hardware must be built for dual channels if this function is used with any channel other than 1. If it is not, this function will assert.

briefdescription.para: Writes to discretes register for the specified GPIO channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an XGpio instance to be worked on.
Contains the channel of the GPIO (1 or 2) to operate on.
Value to be written to the discretes register.

simplesect.para: The hardware must be built for dual channels if this function is used with any channel other than 1. If it is not, this function will assert. See also XGpio_DiscreteSet() and XGpio_DiscreteClear().

briefdescription.para: Initializes the XGpio instance provided by the caller based on the device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: Only InstancePtr is initialized.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an XGpio instance. The memory that the pointer references must be pre-allocated by the caller. Further calls to manipulate the instance/driver through the XGpio API must be made with this pointer.
contains the base address of the device

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the initialization was successful.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the device configuration data was not found for a device with the supplied device ID/BaseAddress.

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XGpio_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xgpio.h for the definition of XGpio_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Set output discrete(s) to logic 1 for the specified GPIO channel.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to an XGpio instance to be worked on.
Channel
Contains the channel of the GPIO (1 or 2) to operate on.
Mask
Set of bits that will be set to 1 in the discrete data register. All other bits in the data register are unaffected.
None.
The hardware must be built for dual channels if this function is used with any channel other than 1. If it is not, this function will assert.
This API can only be used if the GPIO_IO ports in the IP are used for connecting to the external output ports.

briefdescription.para: Set output discrete(s) to logic 0 for the specified GPIO channel.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to an XGpio instance to be worked on.
Channel
Contains the channel of the GPIO (1 or 2) to operate on.
Mask
Set of bits that will be set to 0 in the discrete data register. All other bits in the data register are unaffected.
None. The hardware must be built for dual channels if this function is used with any channel other than 1. If it is not, this function will assert.
This API can only be used if the GPIO_IO ports in the IP are used for connecting to the external output ports.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This function performs a minimal test in which the data register is read. It only does a read without any kind of test because the hardware has been parameterized such that it may be only an input and the state of the input is unknown.
All other hardware features of the device are not guaranteed to be in the hardware since they are parameterizable.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XGpio instance to be worked on. This parameter must be initialized with XGpio_Initialize().

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS always. If the GPIO device was not present in the hardware a bus error is generated. Other indicators of a bus error, such as registers in bridges or buses, may be necessary to determine if this function caused a bus error.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts enabled through XGpio_InterruptEnable() will not be passed through until the global enable bit is set by this function. This function is designed to allow all interrupts (both channels) to be enabled easily for exiting a critical section. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: GPIO instance to operate on.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts enabled through XGpio_InterruptEnable() will no longer be passed through until the global enable bit is set by XGpio_InterruptGlobalEnable(). This function is designed to allow all interrupts (both channels) to be disabled easily for entering a critical section. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: GPIO instance to operate on.

detaileddescription.para: The global interrupt must also be enabled by calling XGpio_InterruptGlobalEnable() for interrupts to occur. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: GPIO instance to operate on.
Mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 are enabled. This mask is formed by OR'ing bits from XGPIO_IR* bits which are contained in xgpio_l.h.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows specific interrupts for each channel to be disabled. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: GPIO instance to operate on.
Mask to disable. Bits set to 1 are disabled. This mask is formed by OR'ing bits from XGPIO_IR* bits which are contained in xgpio_l.h.

briefdescription.para: Clears pending interrupts with the provided mask.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called after the software has serviced the interrupts that are pending. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: GPIO instance to operate on.
Mask to clear pending interrupts. Bit positions of 1 are cleared. This mask is formed by OR'ing bits from XGPIO_IR* bits which are contained in xgpio_l.h.

detaileddescription.para: This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: GPIO instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: A mask of bits made from XGPIO_IR* bits which are contained in xgpio_l.h.

briefdescription.para: Returns the status of interrupt signals.

detaileddescription.para: Any bit in the mask set to 1 indicates that the channel associated with the bit has asserted an interrupt condition. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

InstancePtr
GPIO instance to operate on.
A pointer to a mask of bits made from XGPIO_IR* bits which are contained in xgpio_l.h. The interrupt status indicates the status of the device regardless if the interrupts from the devices have been enabled or not through XGpio_InterruptEnable().

briefdescription.para: Performs a 32-bit write to a GPIO register.

detaileddescription.para: If the GPIO core is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: Base address of the GPIO device.
Register offset from the base to write to.
Data written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XGpio_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Reads a value from a GPIO register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32-bit read is performed. If the GPIO core is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: Base address of the GPIO device.
Register offset from the base to read from.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XGpio_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This section explains the implementation of the basic functionality of the XGpio driver.

detaileddescription.para: The xgpio_sinit.c file contains the implementation of the static initialization functionality of the XGpio driver.
The xgpio_selftest.c file contains implementation of the self test function of the XGpio driver.
This header file contains identifiers and driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
The xgpio_intr.c file contains the implementation of GPIO interrupt processing functions for the XGpio driver.
The xgpio_i.h header file contains internal identifiers, which are shared among the files of the driver.
The xgpio_g.c file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of GPIO devices in the system.
The xgpio_extra.c file contains implementation of the advanced discrete functions of the XGpio driver.
This header file contains the software API definition of the Xilinx General Purpose I/O device driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a rmm 02/04/02 First release 2.00a jhl 12/16/02 Update for dual channel and interrupt support 2.01a jvb 12/13/05 Changed Initialize() into CfgInitialize(), and made CfgInitialize() take a pointer to a config structure instead of a device id. Moved Initialize() into xgpio_sinit.c, and had Initialize() call CfgInitialize() after it retrieved the config structure using the device id. Removed include of xparameters.h along with any dependencies on xparameters.h and the _g.c config table. 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style, added GetDataDirection 2.12a sv 11/21/07 Updated driver to support access through DCR bus 3.00a sv 11/21/09 Updated to use HAL Processor APIs. Renamed the macros to remove _m from the name. 4.1 lks 11/18/15 Clean up of the comments in the code and removed support for DCR bridge 4.2 sk 08/16/16 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress as part of adding 64 bit support. CR# 867425. Changed the prototype of XGpio_CfgInitialize API. 4.9 pm 11/30/21 Addressed Doxygen warning

detaileddescription.para: See xgpio.h for more information about the driver.
These APIs can only be used if the GPIO_IO ports in the IP are used for connecting to the external output ports.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a rmm 02/04/02 First release 2.00a jhl 12/16/02 Update for dual channel and interrupt support 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 3.00a sv 11/21/09 Updated to use HAL Processor APIs. Renamed the macros XGpio_mWriteReg to XGpio_WriteReg, and XGpio_mReadReg to XGpio_ReadReg.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a rmm 02/04/02 First release 2.00a jhl 12/16/02 Update for dual channel and interrupt support 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 4.0 sha 07/15/15 Added XPAR_XGPIO_NUM_INSTANCES macro to control config table parameters.

detaileddescription.para: It is intended for internal use only.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a rmm 03/13/02 First release 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style

detaileddescription.para: See xgpio.h for more information about the driver.
The functions in this file require the hardware device to be built with interrupt capabilities. The functions will assert if called using hardware that does not have interrupt capabilities.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
2.00a jhl 11/26/03 Initial release 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.12a sv 06/05/08 Updated driver to fix the XGpio_InterruptDisable function to properly update the Interrupt Enable register 3.00a sv 11/21/09 Updated to use HAL Processor APIs. Renamed the macros XGpio_mWriteReg to XGpio_WriteReg, and XGpio_mReadReg to XGpio_ReadReg.

detaileddescription.para: The user should refer to the hardware device specification for more details of the device operation.

The macros that are available in this file use a multiply to calculate the addresses of registers. The user can control whether that multiply is done at run time or at compile time. A constant passed as the channel parameter will cause the multiply to be done at compile time. A variable passed as the channel parameter will cause it to occur at run time.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jhl 04/24/02 First release of low level driver 2.00a jhl 11/26/03 Added support for dual channels and interrupts. This change required the functions to be changed such that the interface is not compatible with previous versions. See the examples in the example directory for macros to help compile an application that was designed for previous versions of the driver. The interrupt registers are accessible using the ReadReg and WriteReg macros and a channel parameter was added to the other macros. 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.12a sv 11/21/07 Updated driver to support access through DCR bus. 3.00a sv 11/21/09 Renamed the macros XGpio_mWriteReg to XGpio_WriteReg XGpio_mReadReg to XGpio_ReadReg. Removed the macros XGpio_mSetDataDirection, XGpio_mGetDataReg and XGpio_mSetDataReg. Users should use XGpio_WriteReg/XGpio_ReadReg to achieve the same functionality. 4.1 lks 11/18/15 Removed support for DCR bridge

detaileddescription.para: See xgpio.h for more information about the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a rmm 02/04/02 First release 2.00a jhl 01/13/04 Addition of dual channels and interrupts. 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 3.00a sv 11/21/09 Updated to use HAL Processor APIs.

Ver Who Date Changes
2.01a jvb 10/13/05 First release 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 4.0 sha 07/15/15 Defined macro XPAR_XGPIO_NUM_INSTANCES if not defined in xparameters.h 4.10 gm 07/11/23 Added SDT support. 4.13 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
